title: Lomagramma novoguineensis
taxonomy: Lomagramma novoguineensis is a species of fern in the family Dryopteridaceae. It belongs to the genus Lomagramma and is native to New Guinea. The classification of Lomagramma novoguineensis places it within the kingdom Plantae, phylum Pteridophyta, class Polypodiopsida, and order Polypodiales. This species is also known as the New Guinea Lomagramma fern. Further research may be necessary for a more detailed understanding of its classification.

intro: Lomagramma novoguineensis is a species of fern in the family Dryopteridaceae, native to Papua New Guinea. This plant has been traditionally used in medicine, offering various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may aid in the treatment of wounds, infections, and oxidative stress-related diseases. The bioactive constituents of Lomagramma novoguineensis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and sesquiterpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Lomagramma novoguineensis include teas, infusions, and extracts, which are used to promote wound healing, reduce inflammation, and boost the immune system.
